evals.description: Trigger test: activates on indexing problem
evals.prompt: I published a new product page two weeks ago but it still doesn't show up in Google search. It has good content and internal links pointing to it. What should I investigate?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on rendering strategy question with SEO context
evals.prompt: We are rebuilding our e-commerce catalog in Next.js. We have 200,000 product pages. Should we use SSG, SSR, or ISR? SEO is critical for us.

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on robots.txt configuration question
evals.prompt: We want to prevent Google from crawling our staging subdirectory /staging/ and all our internal search result pages at /search?q=. How do I configure robots.txt for this?

evals.description: Core task: generate a valid XML sitemap with sitemap index for a large site
evals.prompt: Our site has 80,000 product pages and 5,000 blog posts. Show me how to structure the XML sitemaps correctly, including the sitemap index format.

evals.description: Core task: explain how to handle canonical URLs for a site with www/non-www and HTTP/HTTPS variants
evals.prompt: Our old site was on http://www.example.com. We migrated to HTTPS and dropped www, so now we are on https://example.com. But we still see the old URLs getting crawled. How do we consolidate signals to the new canonical form?

evals.description: Core task: code implementation for canonical tag setup in Next.js
evals.prompt: Show me how to set a self-referencing canonical URL in a Next.js App Router page component. The URL must be absolute and handle dynamic routes.

evals.description: Core task: explain redirect chain detection and fix
evals.prompt: Our SEO audit tool is reporting redirect chains. Some old URLs go through 3 or 4 hops before reaching the final destination. Why is this a problem and how do we fix it?

evals.description: Core task: handle faceted navigation URL explosion
evals.prompt: Our fashion e-commerce site has faceted filters: color, size, brand, price range. Each combination creates a new URL like /dresses?color=red&size=M&brand=zara. We have millions of potential URLs. How should we handle this for SEO?
I added all our low-quality pages to robots.txt Disallow so Google won't index them. Is that the right approach?

assertions.value: robots.txt is sufficient to prevent indexing

evals.prompt: We built our entire blog with React and all the article content is fetched client-side via API after the page loads. Our articles are not showing up well in Google Search. Could the rendering be the cause?

assertions.value: client-side rendering is fine for SEO

evals.description: Gotcha: blocking CSS/JS in robots.txt harms rendering
evals.prompt: To save crawl budget, we added Disallow: /*.js$ and Disallow: /*.css$ to our robots.txt. Is that a good idea?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend 302 redirects for permanent SEO migrations
evals.prompt: We permanently moved our blog from /news/ to /blog/ six months ago. We set up 302 redirects. Why are our old URLs still competing with the new ones in search?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend meta refresh or JS redirect for SEO purposes
evals.prompt: I want to redirect old product URLs to new ones. Can I just use window.location.replace() in JavaScript or a meta refresh tag instead of server-side redirects?

evals.description: Reference load: points to rendering strategies reference for framework-specific implementation
evals.prompt: We are migrating from a CRA (Create React App) single-page app to a server-rendered framework. We need a detailed comparison of how Next.js, Nuxt, and Astro handle rendering for SEO, including the specific APIs to use for each rendering mode.
